def1: noun. (‘the bare’) The surface, the (bare) skin.；noun. Surface; body; substance.；adjective. Minimal; that is or are just sufficient.；adjective. Naked, uncovered.；adverb. Very; significantly.；adverb. Barely.
def2: noun. A large omnivorous mammal, related to the dog and raccoon, having shaggy hair, a very small tail, and flat feet; a member of family Ursidae.；noun. A rough, unmannerly, uncouth person.；verb. To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.；adjective. (investments) Characterized by declining prices in securities markets or by belief that the prices will fall.

def1: noun. The shore of a body of water, especially when sandy or pebbly.；noun. A horizontal strip of land, usually sandy, adjoining water.；verb. To run aground on a beach.；verb. To run (something) aground on a beach.；noun. A gravel-filled zone on a racetrack, used as a hazard, exclusionary region, and, safety device to slow down and trap vehicles.
def2: noun. A tree of the genus Fagus having a smooth, light grey trunk, oval, pointed leaves and many branches.；noun. The wood of the beech tree.

def1: noun. A relatively long, wide and thin piece of any material, usually wood or similar, often for use in construction or furniture-making.；noun. A device (e.g., switchboard) containing electrical switches and other controls and designed to control lights, sound, telephone connections, etc.；verb. To step or climb onto or otherwise enter a ship, aircraft, train or other conveyance.；verb. To provide someone with meals and lodging, usually in exchange for money.
def2: noun. A shallow lake, one of a number of bodies of water in eastern Norfolk and Suffolk.；noun. A lathe tool for turning down the insides and bottoms of cylinders.；adjective. Wide in extent or scope.；adjective. Extended, in the sense of diffused; open; clear; full.

def1: noun. A dwelling; habitation; building.
def2: noun. A (usually) metal fastener consisting of a cylindrical body that is threaded, with a larger head on one end. It can be inserted into an unthreaded hole up to the head, with a nut then threaded on the other end; a heavy machine screw.；noun. A sliding pin or bar in a lock or latch mechanism.；verb. To connect or assemble pieces using a bolt.；verb. To secure a door by locking or barring it.；adverb. Suddenly; straight; unbendingly.

def1: verb. To line or finish (a surface, such as a wall), with plaster, stucco, thin boards, or similar.；verb. To set a higher bound.；noun. The overhead closure of a room.；noun. The upper limit of an object or action.
